records.ety: [OF. deforcier; de- or des- (L. de or dis-) + forcier, F. forcer. See Force, v.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deforced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Deforcing.]

defs: To keep from the rightful owner; to withhold wrongfully the possession of, as of lands or a freehold.
To resist the execution of the law; to oppose by force, as an officer in the execution of his duty.
